label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking if following these instructions will bring me back to the starting point. The instructions are: always face forward. Take 8 steps backward, then 2 steps backward, then 10 steps forward. The options are Yes or No. First, I need to understand the directions properly. The key here is the movement in relation to the starting point while facing forward the entire time. When they say "always face forward," that means my facing direction doesn't change during the entire process. I remain facing the same direction throughout all the steps. Now, let's break down the steps: 1. Take 8 steps backward. If I'm facing forward, then "backward" would mean in the opposite direction of where I'm facing. So, these 8 steps would take me 8 units in the opposite direction from my facing direction. 2. Take 2 steps backward. Again, since I'm still facing forward, these 2 steps are in the same "backward" direction as before. So now I've moved 8 + 2 = 10 units in the opposite direction from my facing direction. 3. Take 10 steps forward. Since I'm facing forward, these steps are in the direction I'm facing. So, these 10 steps take me 10 units in the forward direction. So, net movement: - First, I went 10 units in the backward direction (8 + 2). - Then, I went 10 units in the forward direction. So the net movement is 0. I went 10 units in one direction and then 10 units in the opposite direction. Therefore, according to this, I would return to my starting point. Wait, but let me double check. I went 8 backward, then 2 backward, so that's 10 backward, then 10 forward. So
